_comment: MINIMAL teaching sample. A trimmed AnalysisJSON (the Agent 1 / Code Analysis output) for a small fictional CLI. It plausibly produces agent2-script-minimal.json. Conforms to AnalysisJSON in @lenzon/shared-types (packages/shared-types/src/analysis.ts). codeQuality/health are optional and omitted here to keep it small.

notableDependencies.name: commander
notableDependencies.purpose: Parses CLI arguments and dispatches subcommands.

notableDependencies.name: fast-glob
notableDependencies.purpose: Walks the filesystem to find Markdown files.

architecture.summary: A small command-line tool that turns a folder of Markdown notes into a static HTML site. A single entry point parses arguments, a scanner collects files, and a renderer writes HTML. There is no server or database.

entryPoints.role: Parses argv and dispatches to the build command.

modules.name: Scanner
modules.responsibility: Finds Markdown files under the input directory.

modules.name: Renderer
modules.responsibility: Converts Markdown to HTML and writes output files.

plainEnglish.oneLiner: A command-line tool that turns a folder of Markdown notes into a small website.
plainEnglish.fullExplanation: Leaflet CLI is a tiny utility for people who keep notes in Markdown. You point it at a folder and it produces a folder of HTML you can host anywhere. It does one thing: read Markdown, write HTML. There is no account system, no database, and no server — it runs once and exits.

userJourneys.name: First build
userJourneys.narrative: A writer installs the tool, runs it against their notes folder, and gets a ready-to-host site in seconds.
